text: The surgeon introduces an endo bag into the peritoneal cavity, preparing for the laparoscopic cystectomy procedure.
The surgeon opens the endo bag after its introduction into the peritoneal cavity and positions it within the pelvis, preparing for the laparoscopic in-bag cystectomy procedure.
The surgeon places the entire dermoid cyst into the opened endobag positioned within the pelvis, preparing for laparoscopic cystectomy.
The surgeon positions the endo bag along the anti-ovarian border or the anti-mycentric border of the ovary within the pelvic cavity.
The surgeon makes an incision and gently peels the dermoid cyst away from the cyst wall, carefully separating it from surrounding tissue.
The completed cystectomy reveals a bloodless dissection, achieved by maintaining the correct anatomical plane during the procedure.
The surgeon opens the cyst wall inside the endo bag, revealing sebaceous content and hair being expressed from the cyst.
The sebaceous content and hair from the cyst remain contained within the endo bag during the procedure.
The surgeon removes the endobag containing the cyst from the peritoneal cavity, ensuring no spillage occurs during the extraction, marking the completion of the surgical procedure.
